name: Petroglyph H20, Khatm Al Melaha, Kalba, Sharjah
description:
Natural Version with Paint, Petroglyph H20, Khatm Al Melaha, Kalba, Sharjah. One poorly defined animal. Likely Neolithic or earlier. [Fossati 2019 Messages from the Past: Rock Art of the Al-Hajar Mountains (Oman)].

Khatm Al Melaha is an archaeological site on the coast of the Oman Sea near Kalba in Sharjah, UAE. It is one of the largest rock art sites in the UAE.

Over 175 stones with petroglyphs were documented and close to 400 motifs were identified. Every rock with a glyph was given an ID nu

name: Printing matrix for pharmacy advertising
description:
Printing matrix for an advertisement of a pharmacy store of Jadwiga Sikorska-Klemensiewicz

Zinc matrix for printing advertisements. Jadwiga Sikorska-Klemensiewicz's pharmacy in Krakow. 
Jadwiga Sikorska-Klemensiewicz (1871–1963) was one of the first three pharmacy students at the Faculty of Philosophy of the Jagiellonian University. She obtained a Master's Degree in Pharmacy in 1898.

ID no.: KGZ 4916

Time of creation: ca. 1903

Museum: The Museum of Pharmacy at the Jagiellonian University Med

name: Angiosperm: Koelreuteria allenii (Cornell Univ.)
description: Fossil fruit of the flowering plant *Koelreuteria allenii* from the late Eocene Florissant Flora of Florissant, Colorado. The fruits of *Koelreuteria* are inflated and somewhat leaflike in appearance. This specimen shows one segment of an inflated fruit. *Koelreuteria* (Sapindaceae, soapberry family), or golden raintree, is today native to Asia, although it may be seen in cultivation elsewhere. Specimen is from the Cornell University Paleobotanical Collection (CUPC), Ithaca, New York (#1584-5).

name: Rhinoceros skull
description:
Surface scan of the skull of an Indian Rhinoceros from the D’Arcy Thompson Zoology Museum at the University of Dundee.

Indian Rhino - Rhinoceros unicornis. DUNUC 2111 & 2111/1.

Scanned with an Artec Eva surface scanner. The resulting file is exported as an obj. and imported into Zbrush. The texture is captured during the scanning process and converted to Polypaint in Zbrush.

We’d love to hear from you if you download or print our models, especially if you use them in education and outreach.

name: Guilford Projectile Point
description:
A chert Guilford projectile point curated at the Natural History Society of Maryland. The Guilford point is characterized by a long, narrow, thick blade with a straight, rounded, or concave base. This point type dates to the latter part of the Middle Archaic period ranging from 6200 to 5500 BP (approximately 5250-4350 BC).

Courtesy of the Natural History Society of Maryland which has given permission for the model to be downloadable for non-commercial educational purposes.

name: HL-103 Bone Labret, Hot Springs Village, Alaska
description:
Decorated Bone Labret, Hot Springs Village, Port Moller, Alaska CAT# HL-103
Okada excavations HL02. Hot Springs 2B 500-850 CE

The Hot Springs site is a massive village on the shore of Port Moller, on the Alaska Peninsula side of the southern Bering Sea. It was excavated by several different teams over the last 100 years. The main occupations are from 2000 BCE-1000 BCE, and from 100 CE to 800 CE.

The Hot Springs artifacts are presented as a result of the research conducted under grants NSF 0137

name: Roman Coin
description:
Does someone know anything about that coin? It's from my numismatic collection when I was a child. A short research on the web said "Galliemus 253-268 Antoninian" – whatever that means. :)

This is another photogrammetry test. Testing a reproducable setup and procedure. And well, unfortunatelly it wasn't very reproducable. Nevertheless, I got a quite okay-ish model.

Setup: OpenScanner (www.openscan.eu) in a light tent with 3 soft boxes, Sony Alpha 77 II, SAL100M28 (100mm / f2.8 Macro), 4 chunks

name: COVID-19 Infection, Stay Home!
description:
A useful model to explain the importance of staying at home, breaking the link between thousands and saving lives.

**Watch the video here: **

* **[English](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nemSzeqs1lo&t=)** 
* **[Spanish](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yHg2nBNmerA)** 

**Theory & Concept**

* PhD Maia Majumder says this chain of corona virus has a R0 of 3, meaning one person can infect up to 3
* While, the ordinary Flu has a R0* of 1.3, for example
* So one person can have contact and infect u

name: Instructional Chess - Knight
description:
Instructional Chess demonstrates – on the body of each piece – how pieces are allowed to move on the board. Seen from above, squares represent potential "landing" places for each piece, beyond which they may not move, while arrows represent the possibility of continuous movement across the board. 

Each piece was custom designed in virtual reality (Oculus Medium) and 3D printed for proof-of-concept testing. I hope to get this instructional chess set into the hands of local educators, chess clubs

name: N024 Camp fire - Free version
description:
**Clean photogrammetry assets from bimage **

*Model optimization Process* 

- - photoscanning from 20 to 200 pictures depending on object complexity 
- - 3d meshing and texturing with meshroom or reality capture 
- - model cleaning with blender 
- - quad remeshing with instant mesh 
- - texture baking with blender (4K diffuse + normal + roughness + AO + displacement) 
- - exporting FBX, OBJ, DAE. 

**This are optimized assets**; each 3d blender file is from 5 to 20 Mo including the 4K jpg textu

name: Mii Monsieur Plouf
description:
La chaîne de Monsieur Plouf: https://www.youtube.com/user/ChroniquesMrPlouf/videos

Au début, je me demandais comment j'allais faire la coiffure...et puis j'ai pris la coiffure de Joker de Persona 5 !
Ensuite, j'ai remarqué que faire un Monsieur Plouf cubique avec la coiffure était impossible... du coup,j'ai repris le modèle 3D de base des personnages Mii !
Pour finir, j'ai dessiné de tout arrondir sur le visage afin de ne pas faire quelque chose d'effrayant !

name: Ceomi - Woman dancing.
description:
Another attempt at making a low-poly character for mobile games. This character was an experiment to see how well the topology deformed when using auto-rigging tools (ActorCore AccuRIG) and premade animations.

I like using dance animations, because it's possible to spot a lot of animation or auto-rigging issues early on.

author: BloedSkrif

name: Petroglifo Taboexa - 2 Panel  2
description:
Los petroglifos de A Coutada fueron descubiertos tras los incendios de 2017.

Tras el hallazgo del petroglifo, los arqueólogos fueron a visitarlo. Los expertos quedaron maravillados al ver que no se trataba de dibujos de ciervos, como suele ser habitual, sino de figuras de équidos. De este modo pudieron confirmar “un nuevo paradigma en el arte rupestre gallego y portugués” sobre el que llevaban dos años trabajando: la gran roca inclinada existente en Taboexa muestra pequeñas cavidades repartidas
faiss_text:
Petroglifo Taboexa - 2 Panel  2 | Los petroglifos de A Coutada fueron descubiertos tras los incendios de 2017.

Tras el hallazgo del petroglifo, los arqueólogos fueron a visitarlo. Los expertos quedaron maravillados al ver que no se trataba de dibujos de ciervos, como suele ser habitual, sino de figuras de équidos. De este modo pudieron confirmar “un nuevo paradigma en el arte rupestre gallego y portugués” sobre el que llevaban dos años trabajando: la gran roca inclinada existente en Taboexa muestra pequeñas cavidades repartidas | petroglifo cultural-heritage arte-rupestre heritage-photogrammetry | art-abstract cultural-heritage-history | animal art_sculpture

name: Framecad B2B Strong Axis LC System Truss
description:
FRAMECAD

* Thomas Delamore

* Daniel Spencer

One issue currently faced with LC System trusses involves the sharp edges of each truss member being exposed. This project was aimed at improving safety features of back-to-back truss designs by chamfering the edges of each member. 

Link to Truss Drawings (PDF):

* https://drive.google.com/file/d/1aHJJCgrDm-rVHeyASQvsy3yXfzxKDwkD/view?usp=sharing

name: Trilobite fossil
description:
Trilobites (class Trilobita) are a group of extinct marine animals that first appeared in the Early Cambrian perion (521 million years ago). They are the earliest-known group of arthropods and among the most successful of all early animals, existing in Earth's oceans for almost 300 million years (until the end of the Permian 252 million years ago).

More info here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trilobite

Accession #: 108.04

Captured with Artec Spider 3D Scanner 
https://www.artec3d.com/portabl

name: The Altar Preetz
description:
The Altar Preetz, central panel, ca 1435, Hamburg (Germany). The National Museum (Copenhagen, Denmark). Made by Memento Beta.

The altarpiece originally had two pairs of wings, but only one survives. All the carved figures display their original, well- preserved colours and gilding. The altarpiece was made ca. 1435 by a pupil from Master Francke's workshop in Hamburg, Germany. Preetz Abbey, Holstein.

name: Ameer - Model
description:
Unfortunately, I was unable to use a 3D scanner to capture a model of my body, so I instead used a tripod to take 4 photos of myself:

1. In front view (me head on looking into the camera)
2. Back view (behind me)
3. Side/Profile (my right and left side)
4. Back Side/Profile

After that, I made some adjustments to the photos using photoshop to white balance them, allowing me to grab all the necessary details I needed to depict the shape of my body.

### Modeling Myself
After the prep work was do

name: 3December 2020 - "Optical Illusion"
description:
I apologize for the appaling quality of the humour.

A replica of a TOSLINK connector and cable, which should be very close to the actual standard. Contrary to popular belief, gold plating your optical connectors is inconsequential to the transmission of light.

## Tools used
* Modelling and baking in Blender
* Texturing in GIMP
* Textures sourced from Textures.com

description:
A model detailing a 3D representation of a stereographic projection for the Hermann–Mauguin group 3m.

Special thanks to Alex M. Pavelic for his help in making these models.

These models were made as part of an educational series for helping to visualize the theory behind crystallography.  To find other models made in this set, please search for the tag “Crystallography_Education”

Additionally, we thank the NSF-GRFP for funding via DGE-1747503.

name: Stela 6 Aguateca
description:
This 3D model is of Stela 6 from Aguateca, Mayan administrative and ceremonial center located in the lowlands of southern Yucatán. The triumphant figure in the relief is a warrior dressed in a headdress of the underworld jaguar god. 

The 21 photographs used to compose the model were taken with a Samsung Galaxy S8+ in January 2016 at the anthropology museum in Santiago, Chile. The images were processed in Argisoft PhotoScan Professional, version 1.2.3.

name: Anguru Salt Glacier, Zagros Mountains, Iran
description:
The Anguru Salt Glacier is formed by a salt diapir that pierced the crest of a fault-cored anticline in Iran's southernmost Zagros Mountains.
No vertical exaggeration.

Reading suggestion: 
Sarkarinejad K, Sarshar MA, Adineh S (2018) Structural, micro-structural and kinematic analyses of channel flow in the Karmostaj salt diapir in the Zagros foreland folded belt, Fars province, Iran J Struct Geol 107:109-131, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jsg.2017.12.005

Generator: DEM Net Elevation API - https://

name: LLGRID6 (2014) low-res
description:
**Aeolian soil erosion, Kangerlussuaq, West Greenland**  
*June/July 2014*  

We are researching erosional and ecological processes in these *deflation patches*, which cover 22% of the local landscape. Katabatic winds off the Greenland Ice Sheet remove fine-grained loess soil, exposing glacial till. The patch edge advances as soil and vegetation are undercut. Within patches, a biological soil crust forms, hindering future erosion and aiding soil recovery.   
  
**People**  
Ruth Heindel, Earth S

name: Star Wars World
description:
This experience is meant to be viewed in AR/VR. Use an approprate AR/VR headset to see what the planet looks like from the perspetive of the visitors.

Why see one planet when you can see the most iconic Star Wars planets all in one? From Tatooine, Dagobah, Mustafar, Naboo, Cloud City, Hoth, and the first Death Star, you'll be able to explore each environment. Plus, keep an eye out for notable figures, machines, spacecrafts, and biomes in each planet ;)

name: Norðurfjörður - Tækni og vísinda svæði
description:
The seccond sector in Norðurfjörður, right side of Sögu svæði. The clifs are more than 30m at the tallest point. Here there is a huges roof that waits for someone to check it out.

Here there are 6 bolted routes, one trad route and two routes in the making. Along with loads of potential for new routes.

In the summer of 2020 the first 5.12 in Norðurfjörður was opened right here.

Here you can find information on all the routes: https://www.klifur.is/crag/nordurfjordur

The model is made from a v

name: Verteris Roman Fort / Brough Castle
description: The Roman fort of Verteris was constructed on the highest part of a ridge on the south bank of Swindale Beck, a tributary of the River Eden, slightly downstream of its confluence with Augill Beck. It lies at the western end of the Stainmore Pass, a route followed by the main Roman road through the Pennines that connects the Roman legionary fortress at York (Eboracum) with the fort at Carlisle (Luguvalium). The occupation of the fort possibly dates from the governorship of Agricola (AD 78-84) to

Old refectory room of the Diocesan Museum of Tarragona. Cathedral of Tarragona.
208 x 65 x 12 cm

The nine muses:
1. Clio: Discovered history and guitar.
2. Euterpe: Discovered several musical instruments, courses and dialectic.
3. Thalia: She was the protector of comedy.
4. Melpomene: Opposite from Thalia,

name: Head of a Votary from the Temple of Golgoi
description:
Helmeted and bearded head of a votary of carved limestone; in the archaic Cypriote style; the beard radiating with intersections; the helmet is quilted and framed and allows one row of curls to show above forehead. Ears are showing; a vigorous representation in nearly perfect state of preservation. From the temple of Golgoi. ;votary in the temple of Golgai; head only; archaic, cypriote style; radiating beard, quilted helmet; archaic smile; strong frontality.

name: Artefato 3 com Reconstrução do Acabamento
description:
Essa simulação 3D foi baseada em uma ponta de flecha do acervo do Museu Universitário de Arqueologia e Etnologia (MUAE) da Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul (UFRGS). Modelo feito por João Vinícius Chiesa Back, estudante de História da UFRGS e participante do Centro de Tecnologia Acadêmica (CTA). A partir do artefato arqueológio, foi elaborada a reconstrução do seu acabamento.

Dados do Objeto Datação: 7 - 6 mil anos atrás. Local de Achado: Rio Grande do Sul, região da Campanha, atual Ale

name: Stittenham Pictish Wolf Stone
description:
This fragment of a fine 7th century AD Pictish symbol stone was found built into an old wall at Stittenham, Ardross, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland at some point prior to 1863.  It was found with a second fragment of decorated stone that depicts a beast (https://skfb.ly/6nxLB).  This stone is 0.31m high by 0.49m wide and 0.13m thick.

For more information on this stone see [CANMORE](http://canmore.org.uk/site/13738) and the [Catalogue of Pictish Art in Inverness Museum](http://her.highland.gov.uk/h

name: Grand arrowhead
description:
Made with 3df zephyr aerial, using 77 photos.  
This point was found on a lakeshore, where the surrounding soil had been erroded by animal traffic and water.
It's in really great condition, since it hasn't ever been hit with farm equipment.  The material is a coarse hardstone, similar to granite - the only one in my collection not made from some form of flint, chert, or limestone.
collection ID: p45.

name: Charmeleon (Pokemon)
description:
Charmeleon is a bipedal, reptilian creature. It has bright-red scales and a cream underside. There is a horn-like protrusion on the back of its head, and it has narrow blue eyes and a long snout. It has relatively long arms with three sharp claws. Its short legs have feet with three claws and cream-colored soles. The tip of its long, powerful tail has a flame burning on it. The temperature rises to unbearable levels if Charmeleon swings its tail.

name: Graffiti in Dwarfie Stane, Hoy, Orkney
description:
The stone 'pillow' in the side compartment of the Dwafie Stone has attracted a considerable amount of graffiti over the years.  The earliest is 'H Ross' dated 1735. One notable engraving is that of Hugh Miller, the famous geologist, who visited in 1846.  This monument is now a Scheduled Ancient Monument and it is illegal to deface it in any way.

For a complete model of the Dwarfie Stane see https://skfb.ly/IIYS

name: Czar radio receiver
description: The Czar (Polish for “Charm”) mono radio receiver was launched in 1960. In terms of the electrical system, it is a battery powered superheterodyne, comprising seven transistors and two germanium diodes. The maximum running time of the radio is between 60 and 120 hours. The radio operates in the medium and long wave bands, with amplitude modulation (AM). The receiver has a built-in ferrite antenna and a magnetoelectric speaker. The cuboid, relatively flat housing of the receiver is made of plasti
faiss_text: Czar radio receiver | The Czar (Polish for “Charm”) mono radio receiver was launched in 1960. In terms of the electrical system, it is a battery powered superheterodyne, comprising seven transistors and two germanium diodes. The maximum running time of the radio is between 60 and 120 hours. The radio operates in the medium and long wave bands, with amplitude modulation (AM). The receiver has a built-in ferrite antenna and a magnetoelectric speaker. name: Equus neogeus – tooth
description:
This is a 3D laser scan of part of a molar tooth of *Equus neogeus*. It is in two parts (the other part is specimen number NHMUK PV M 16558 b). The tooth was collected in 1832-1833 at Punta Alta in Argentina by Charles Darwin and is between 129,000 and 11,700 years old. It is part of the Earth Sciences collection at the Natural History Museum, London. This has been scanned as part of the Museum’s Darwin Fossil Mammals digitisation project. Find out more at www.nhm.ac.uk/darwinsfossils.

name: Azara's Domed Armadillo (NHMW-Zoo1-MAMM)
description:
3D model based on a microCT scan of a frozen Southern three-banded armadillo, also known as Azara’s domed armadillo (*Tolypeutes matacus*). These animals live in South America and can be found in Bolivia, Brazil, Argentina and Paraguay. They can be as long as 25 centimeters and weigh up to 2 kilograms. It is the only armadillo species that can roll up into a complete ball.

This particular armadillo will be mounted by our taxidermists to be part of an upcoming exhibition at the NHMW in 2022.

**

name: Monumentul Armatei Franceze a Dunării
description:
Primul Război Mondial transformă orașul Giurgiu (reședință a județului Vlașca și port al Capitalei la Dunăre) în teatru de război. În 1916, Giurgiu a fost ocupat de către armatele Puterilor Centrale (contingente germane, austro-ungare, bulgare și turce), jefuit și incendiat, suferind pierderi foarte de mari (cca. 50% dintre locuințe au fost distruse).
După doi ani de la invazie, la 10 noiembrie 1918, orașul a fost eliberat de Armata Franceză a Dunării, condusă de generalul M.M. Berthelot.

Monum

Tiene una forma cerrada, parte de un labio redondeado y borde con orientación semi vertical que continúa en un cuello estrecho y un hombro curvilíneo cóncavo. El cuerpo es recto y la base plana. description:
Model produced as part of an unmanned aerial photogrammetric survey of the Ledbeg area of Assynt, in NW Scotland. Central Assynt contains the remains of at least 46 Neolithic and Bronze Age cairns and there is potential for further discoveries.

Includes a possible Bronze Age barrow, see annotation.

Survey area 791m x 322m, 25.3 hectares, model created 16/02/2017, resolution 3.3 cm/px, camera DJI Inspire 1 X3.

Taken from the game's files and optimized and prepared for FDM 3D Printing.
Abductors hover over certain places and... kind of just stay put. But only for a little while. After some time, it’ll send beams at the ground, taking away those who decide to walk in them. Some who claim to have been abducted say they were taken to the Mothership, but we don’t know what the Aliens would have to gain. Why let people see the Mothership then bring them back home?

name: Model 51 - Kemps Ridley Sea Turtle (with ID)
description:
This is a 3D model (“Tumbleweed”) of a juvenile Kemps Ridley Sea Turtle (ID 132) (*Lepidochelys kempii*) that was initially rescued by the New England Aquarium in Massachusetts, and then photographed with the Beastcam technology with 30 Canon G16 cameras. The sea turtle was released soon after in the Southern United States in good condition. The 3D model was initially created through a photogrammetry scan using Capturing Reality.

Animator Robert Gutierrez used Blender to reconstruct the full se

name: Maqueta en la Roca, Cupo.
description:
Esta maqueta, tallada en la roca, corresponde a otra versión algo diferente a la que encontrarás en esta página (maqueta en la roca, altiplano chileno). 
Se encuentra ubicada en el poblado de Cupo, en la región de Antofagasta. 
El rol que este tipo de expresión jugo en las sociedades prehispánicas aun no es del todo claro, sin embargo, es posible correspondan a la representación de hitos del paisaje en relación a sus actividades o practicas (campos de cultivo, manejo del agua, reproducción).

name: Laser Air Nozzle 43mm Spindle Adapter Opt Lasers
description:
This file contains a Laser Air Nozzle 43 mm Spindle Adapter, which is a device designed in order to direct the airflow from the fan into the focus of an engraving and cutting laser. Without appropriate airflow, the sample may develop defects such as uneven cutting depth or different colour of the engravement during the laser engraving or cutting procedure. The nozzle is designed to work with Opt Lasers engraving and cutting laser heads. This file is also accessible on the following website:

name: Organic Model
description: I started with a box model and added loops and edges where needed to shape out my model. After I had basic features of the model created I started to subdivide the model more and pulled around areas with soft selection. Finally, I used Maya's sclupting tools to create more fine tuned details. Although this is a pretty low detail model, it is able to get the point of what an organic model is. The difference between hard surface and organic modeling is that you must use a higher poly count to crea

description: Studzienka (rząp, beczka) – publiczny zbiornik z wodą pitną zlokalizowany w sąsiedztwie obecnej ul. Grodzkiej 16, na dawnym placu przy farze lubelskiej pw Św. Michała.  Studzienka miała średnicę około 1,2 m i wysokość około 1 m. miała kształt beczki ściśniętej na dole obręczą metalową (4), a od góry obręczami drewnianymi (8). Woda do beczki prowadzana była drewniana rurą zasilającą (1). W jej górnej części były 2 otwory. W pierwszym znajdował się drewniany korek tzw. klapka (3) przez który woda

name: Addax Voxel
description:
An Addax model that I made using reference images online. Please give credit if used. 33x13x30

The [addax](https://www.livescience.com/55277-addax.html) is a species of antelope. Addaxes are also known as white antelopes and the screwhorn antelopes. They are critically endangered; there may be only three remaining in the wild. Extinction may not be imminent, however; about 2,000 are kept in zoos and on ranches around the world.

name: Monolito Edafológico Nº3
description:
Se trata de un perfil compuesto por dos suelos. El superficial que llega hasta los 26 cm de profundidad se clasifica como Cambisol háplico (eútrico, nóvico). El suelo enterrado que se desarrolla a partir de los 26 cm de profundidad podríamos clasificarlo como Regosol háplico (eútrico).

Este suelo se ha formado debido a la aportación de materiales en una ladera inclinada. Se trata de una pequeña depresión que ha recibido importantes aportes de la erosión de los suelos de las partes más altas.

name: Aachhorn / Pilgrim’s Horn
description:
***Aachhorn***

*Mansfeld, Luthers Elternhaus*

*um 1500*

*Irdenware*

*L 10,5 cm (Originalfragment); L ges. 35 cm*

>Landesamt für Denkmalpflege und Archäologie Sachsen-Anhalt

Dieses Signalhorn aus Pfeifenton war ein Andenken an eine Pilgerreise nach Aachen in Westdeutschland. Der Dom der Stadt beherbergt noch heute die Sammlung an textilen Reliquien, die zu Luthers Zeit für die wichtigste in Europa gehalten wurde.
_____________________
***Pilgrim’s Horn***

*Mansfeld, Luther’s parents’ home*

name: Bowl with conical base and receding rim
description:
It concerns a type 2 vessel in the typology of the El Argar culture of the Siret brothers, excavated in the southeast of Spain. These hand shaped bowls with conical base come from funerary contexts, where they are often found together with type 8 vessels. This type of ceramic is characterized by a dark, polished, metallic surface. (Argaric culture, 2300 BC - 1600 BC). Inv n.: PG.2000.1449

Find this object in the museum’s online catalog [Carmentis](https://www.carmentis.be:443/eMP/eMuseumPlus?se

name: Avocuddling
description:
The avocado (Persea americana), a tree likely originating from south-central Mexico, is classified as a member of the flowering plant family Lauraceae. The fruit of the plant, also called an avocado (or avocado pear or alligator pear), is botanically a large berry containing a single large seed. Avocado trees are partially self-pollinating, and are often propagated through grafting to maintain predictable fruit quality and quantity

Avocados are cultivated in tropical and Mediterranean climates

name: XFP-056 Burnishing tool, Sanak Island, Alaska
description:
Bone burnishing tool, Sanak Island, Alaska. CAT# XFP-056-4-1

XFP-056 is a group of large house depressions on the south shore of Pauloff Harbor, Sanak Island, Alaska. Multiple radiocarbon dates place it from 300 CE to 800 CE, although the upper most levels may date to the 13th century.

These artifacts were scanned with either a Faro Edge Arm or a Minolta Vivid 9i. Processed in Geomagic or Polyworks. 4-8 photos were used for texture in ZBrush. The Sanak Island artifacts are presented as a resul

name: The Great Kiva at Casa Malpais
description:
This structure is a large ceremonial room at the ancient village now called Casa Malpais, near the Town of Springerville Arizona. The structure was probably built in the late thirteeth century as part of the western Pueblo tradition. Great Kivas such as these were present at most villages in the Mogollon highlands during this period of time.

This village is open for guided tours, check in with the Casa Malpais Musuem at the Springerville Hertiage Center, 418 E Main St, Springervile, AZ.

Quartz porhyry is a type of volcanic (igneous) rock containing large porphyritic crystals of quartz.
The porphyry deposits are formed by differentiation and cooling of a column of rising magma in stages.

name: Harpoceras
description:
*Harpoceras*  (Ammonoidea, Ammonitida).
Toarciano (Giurassico Inferiore), cosmopolita.
Conchiglia moderatamente involuta con ombelico profondo, molto compressa con sezione del giro ellittica e ventre unicarenato. Ornata da coste semplici falcoidi molto ravvicinate e munita di rostro ed apofisi iugali. Sutura ammonitica con numerosi lobi ausiliari.
L'esemplare rappresentato è conservato in parte come conchiglia e in parte come modello interno.
faiss_text:

name: KOSMOS Maltese Cross 66-25 V12
description:
The KOSMOS project aim to develop a rotating observation sub-marine system.
It is intended to become a tool for a participatory science project.
The project was initiated by a partnership between the Konk Ar Lab and 
scientists of the French Institute of the sea.
This is the second version.

This post shows the Maltese Cross for the Malta Crodss locked parallel gearbox.
Updated models will be published here.
Mechanics (concept, parts and assembly) of the Kosmos © 2021 by Christophe SERGENT are l

name: #SculptJanuary18 DAY 3 Excitement
description:
Submission for Day 3 of #SculptJanuary18 !

About the submission: So naturally falling behind schedule with my Sculpting, I got a bit depressed the other day and didn't feel like doing anything. It doesn't help either that I've left a lot of friends behind to carry on my work and basically have zero distractions whilst I complete my craft. Still, it gets lonely and I will have off days. I decided to play catch up today, here is Excitement, although technically I am a "day ahead" of SculptJanuary

name: Petroglyph N2 Khatm Al Melaha, Kalba, Sharjah
description:
Natural Version, Petroglyph N2 Khatm Al Melaha, Kalba, Sharjah. Two Syrian Wild Ass. Likely Neolithic or earlier. [Fossati 2019 Messages from the Past: Rock Art of the Al-Hajar Mountains (Oman)].

Khatm Al Melaha is on the coast of the Oman Sea near Kalba in Sharjah, UAE. It is one of the largest rock art sites in the UAE.

Over 175 stones with petroglyphs were documented and close to 400 motifs were identified. Every rock with a glyph was given an ID number and a GPS coordinate. In total 25455

name: Photogrammetry/retopology - Cherub
description:
This is my second work in photogrammetry after the classes in CocoSchool. The workflow was:
- Example photos from https://www.3dflow.net/technology/documents/3df-zephyr-tutorials/
- Photogrammetry in Agisoft Photoscan ( with CocoSchool computer).
- Manual retopology and UVs in Maya
- Albedo from LowPoly again in Photoscan
- Normals and and material in Substance Painter (4k).

name: Not a Black Hole - 1
description:
Given the recent scientific news and the fact that I'm on the "experimenting with volumetrics" path, I figured out that trying to reproduce space objects with simple force-driven particle simulation could be a fun exercise. Here are some first tests !

This object was made by moving 1000 particles around (rigid body rotation initial speed, one central repulsive force and two attractive ones), and "registering" their paths in an alpha voxels grid (225x225x225), which I finally mapped to textured

name: A twin capital with decoration of plant motifs
description:
The presented twin capital is one of four such column capitals discovered over the years 1947–1953 during archaeological research carried out while reconstructing the eastern wing of the monastery.
The double capitals in the collections of the Museum of the Benedictine Abbey in Tyniec are among the best examples of Romanesque masonry in Poland. They come from the first monastic foundation in Tyniec. However, researchers do not agree whether the twin capitals were originally located in the church

name: Kaiser Joseph II.
description: Model of the Emperor Joseph II. monument in the park of Schönbrunn Palace. The equestrian statue by Franz Anton Zauner dates from 1797 and has been in the palace park since 1808. It served as a model for the equestrian statue of Emperor Joseph II on Josefsplatz in the Hofburg, which was unveiled in 1807. It took 10 years from the design to the finished monument. The model for the equestrian statue was the monument of Marcus Aurelius.

name: Biface Fragment XCB-003-116
description:
Biface Fragment XCB-003-116

XCB-003, or IZM-003, is a site tested by A.P. McCartney in 1971. It is the type-site for the Izembek Phase. A small village with a unique whalebone house and many artifacts similar to those in the Bristol Bay region. It dates approximately 1200-1350 CE.

The Western Alaska Peninsula artifacts are presented as a result of the research conducted under grants NSF 9630072, NSF 9814086, NSF 9996372, NSF 9996415, NSF 1139266, NSF 1321411. H. Maschner, Principal Investigato

name: Modified Sea Mammal Bone XCB-105-4124
description:
Modified Sea Mammal Bone XCB-105-4124. 400 BCE-100 CE.

XCB-105 Adamagan (Aleut for place of walrus hunters) is at the head of Morzhovoi Bay, western Alaska Peninsula. It is a massive village with multiple occupations. When it was occupied 400 BCE-100 CE, it was the largest village in the Arctic with an estimated 1000 people. It also has limited occupations dated 2200-1700 BCE, 1000-600 BCE, and 900-1100 CE.

The Western Alaska Peninsula artifacts are presented as a result of the research conduc

name: Echinopora lamellosa (NHMW-Zoo3-EV15251)
description:
3D scan of the calcium carbonate skeleton of a stony coral (*Echinopora lamellosa*). *Echinopora lamellosa* can be found across the tropical Indo-Pacific ocean, down to depths of 35 meters. IUCN (International Union for Conservation of Nature) attributes this species a conservation status of Least Concern. 

This specimen, originated from Samoa, was scanned as part of a pilot study to identify new morphological traits for species delimitation, and it is not on display in the showrooms of the NHM

name: Killeshin Romanesque Door
description:
The west gable of Killeshin has a central Romanesque doorway of four receding orders beneath a pointed-arch hood mould. The arch voussoirs are decorated with human, animal, foliage and geometric motifs, and the capitals feature human heads with inter-twined hair. A granite font stands to the north of the doorway.
![Funded by ](https://www.heritagecouncil.ie/content/files/HERITAGECOUNCILlogo.jpg)

name: Petroglyph C25 Khatm Al Melaha, Kalba, Sharjah
description:
Natural Version, Petroglyph C25 Khatm Al Melaha, Kalba, Sharjah. Three human feet or foot prints. Likely Neolithic or earlier. [Fossati 2019 Messages from the Past: Rock Art of the Al-Hajar Mountains (Oman)].

Khatm Al Melaha is an archaeological site on the coast of the Oman Sea near Kalba in Sharjah, UAE. It is one of the largest rock art sites in the UAE.

Over 175 stones with petroglyphs were documented and close to 400 motifs were identified. Every rock with a glyph was given an ID number a

name: Fruit Basket
description:
A basket is a container that is traditionally constructed from stiff fibers and can be made from a range of materials, including wood splints, runners, and cane. While most baskets are made from plant materials, other materials such as horsehair, baleen, or metal wire can be used. Baskets are generally woven by hand. Some baskets are fitted with a lid, while others are left open on top.
**FILE FORMAT:**
1.stl

author: Alf Does Stuff

name: Photo Scanned Bread #04
description:
This model was made by close-range photogrammetry.

Reconstructed from high-quality photos and processed by Agisoft. ColorChecker was used to achieve the correct representation of colours from the real world to sRGB.

This model was optimised using retopology.

We also created a new effective unwrap UVs, that allowed creating 4k normal maps, achieved by the correct transfer of textures from high poly to low poly.

name: Antique World War II German Telephone
description:
# Antique German Telephone From The 2nd World War

Fully opaque model consists of 17,744 vertices or 34,578 tris.
Handset is 0.23 units in length for reference.

One material and three corresponding 4,096x4,096 textures are supplied:

* Albedo
* Occlusion + Roughness + Metallic
* Normal (Y+ Up)

Modeled in [**Blender 2.92**](http://blender.org) and textured using [**Substance Painter 2019**](https://www.substance3d.com/products/substance-painter/).

A fragment of a Roman cup was discovered by a metal detectorist working with the Ouse and Derwent Project on the site of our 2018 excavation at North Duffield. It was red *terra sigillata*, 'Samian ware' that was imported from Central Gaul in modern day France.

Another distinctive sherd of Samian ware was recovered during the excavation, part of a flanged bowl. Both probably date to the 2nd century AD and are characteristically fine Roman table wares.

name: F. Wertheim & Co. passenger lift cabin
description: Devices for the vertical transport of people and cargo have been known since ancient times. They consist mainly of a platform or cabin for the passengers or cargo, a rope with block and tackle, and a counterweight to the load being transported. Over time, the driving force in the lifting devices has been provided by animals, then steam, and finally the electric motor. The passenger lift cabin presented here, made of wood on a steel frame, was used in the Press Palace on Wielopole street in Krakó
faiss_text: F. Wertheim & Co. passenger lift cabin | Devices for the vertical transport of people and cargo have been known since ancient times. They consist mainly of a platform or cabin for the passengers or cargo, a rope with block and tackle, and a counterweight to the load being transported. Over time, the driving force in the lifting devices has been provided by animals, then steam, and finally the electric motor. The passenger lift cabin presented here, made of wood on a steel frame, was used in the Press Palace on Wielopole street in Krakó | lift transport cabin wertheim | science-technology | nature architecture infrastructure

name: Motorcycle Wheel - Artec Eva 3D Scan
description:
3D scan of the rear wheel from a 1971 Honda CL350 Scrambler. Done with the Artec Eva in HD mode and Artec Studio 15. Mesh simplified from 28 million triangles to 2 million before texturing, but no other edits were made. To see the complete motorcycle scan, visit: https://skfb.ly/6VTC7

To see how we scanned this part, watch this video: https://youtu.be/anagbVZGrec

For more information on the scanner, please visit: https://gomeasure3d.com/artec/eva/

name: Walther G41
description:
Looking for a semi-automatic battle rifle, the German High Command asked Mauser and Walther firms to produce one under strict caracteristics. Finally, the Walther firm would earn the production with the Gewerh 1941 (G41).

This is my favorite semi auto rifle from the World War 2. It was pleasant to work on this model, hope it's fairly accurate.

April 2017

name: Sanderling and Polar Sea Star
description:
The Sanderling (*Calidris alba*) is a small wading sea bird that lives on the Canadian and American coasts. These small birds breed in the high arctic, but migrate down to warmer coasts during the winter. Although the beak is not represented accurately above, Sanderlings use their relatively long beaks to fish buried invertebrates out of the sand for food. 

The Polar Sea Star (*Leptasterias polaris*) is a six armed sea star the lives in the cold waters of the northwest Atlantic Ocean. Small and

A petroleum lamp. 

200 images for reconstruction. 
First try to create a low-poly model and bake textures in Blender ... 
not really very low poly...

Baking textures and "retopo" to low poly in Blender is really a p.i.t.a. imo :D

name: Clockwise Brushless Motor
description:
Clockwise Brushless motor modeled after the Energy Propel 4108-40 A motor. As a device, it is built to drive propellers with diameters of 13-15". It has a built-in ESC allowing easy connection to an MCU and its motor characteristics are 390 KV.

A single 14" propeller is attached as well as associated prop adapters. The actual motor comes with different adapters that are slightly different in size.

https://hitecrcd.com/products/electronic-speed-controls/multirotor-motors/energy-propel-410840-in

name: Psittaciformes
description: 鸚形目（學名：Psittaciformes）在動物分類學上是鳥綱中的一個目。本目包括大約342種，通常劃分成鳳頭鸚鵡科和鸚鵡科兩個科。俗語鸚鵡、鸚哥可以用來單獨指代鸚鵡科，或者整個鸚形目，一般多指稱後者。本目鳥類可以在世界上多數氣候溫暖的地區找到，包括印度、東南亞和西非。美國也曾有一個種卡羅琳鸚哥，但現已滅絕。鸚形目為數最多的地區為大洋洲（澳洲）、南美洲和中美洲。
